name: Glaze Recipes
summary: A recipe book of glazes — normalized recipe, cone, surface — grouped by fired result.
why: For ceramicists iterating on glazes: keep every tested recipe (normalized to 100, with cone and surface) in one place, grouped by fired result, so you stop re-deriving the same base. Built because scattered notes and spreadsheets lose the thread across firings.
author: kumare_agape

name: Recipe Normalizer
summary: Normalize any technical recipe to a base of 100, with additives as a percentage, and export a clean Markdown table.
why: Glazes, clay bodies, cocktails, chemicals, paints — any recipe expressed as a ratio benefits from a common base. This turns a rough ingredient list into a normalized, comparable table in one paste, so you can eyeball two recipes side by side without doing the math by hand each time.
author: kumare_agape

name: Ceramic Product
summary: A Current Blueprint template for ceramic pieces headed to production — clay, glaze, firing, packaging.
why: A ceramic product has a fixed set of decisions that, once made, define the piece: clay body, glaze, firing schedule, packaging. This blueprint keeps them pinned as one focal point instead of scattered across notes, so you always know what's actually decided versus still open.
author: kumare_agape
